name: Binary Search
def: Finds a target in a sorted sequence by halving the search interval each step.
problem: Locate an element (or insertion point) in O(log n) over sorted, randomly-accessible data.
mechanism: Compare against the midpoint and discard the half that cannot contain the target; repeat until found or the interval is empty.
limits: Poor locality on huge arrays vs. interpolation/B-tree
Off-by-one and overflow bugs are classic
failures: Incorrect boundary updates causing infinite loops
whenNot: When a hash lookup suffices and order is irrelevant
myths: "Trivial to get right" — boundary handling is famously error-prone
